meaning: adj.未被看到的,不在视野中的,未装瞄准器的
examples: In blind people all this brain power would go to waste, but somehow an unsighted person's brain rewires itself to connect auditory regions of the brain to the visual cortex.
Rare is the pundit discussion about the financial order which leaves this rare bird un-cited, or for that matter unsighted.
West Ham were well on top by now and the unsighted Hart did well to push away Hayden Mullins's curling shot soon afterwards.
Nick Hardwick, chief inspector of prisons, said Highpoint provided a "generally safe and decent environment" but managers were "unsighted on some areas of concern".
